title: Why Wall Street Suddenly Sees SpaceX as an AI Company
excerpt: SpaceX shares have slumped about 40% since its IPO, but analysts increasingly view Starlink as AI infrastructure, with its Aug. 6 earnings report set to test...
content_html:
Key Points

  • Interested in SpaceX? Here are five stocks we like better.

  • SpaceX shares have fallen about 40% since their June 12 IPO and now trade below the $135 listing price.

  • Wall Street is increasingly framing SpaceX's Starlink network as AI infrastructure rather than just a space or satellite business.

  • SpaceX's first public earnings report on Aug. 6 is seen as pivotal for validating the AI narrative amid mixed analyst price targets.

When SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) went public just over a month ago, on June 12, it did so as the most hotly anticipated listing in years, and unmistakably as a space company. Rockets, satellites, and Mars ambitions were the story. But barely a month into its life on the public markets, a different narrative is taking hold on Wall Street, and it has far more to do with artificial intelligence (AI) than with space travel.

The timing is interesting because the stock itself has had a rough start. After hitting a post-IPO high in the sessions following its IPO, SpaceX shares have slumped around 40% and are now trading below the $135 price at which they listed.

→ Buyback Boom: These 3 Companies Are Betting Billions on Their Own Stocks

For a company that generated so much excitement coming to market, dipping below the IPO price inside the first few weeks isn't a great look. Yet beneath that disappointing price action, the emerging investment case may be more compelling than the chart suggests.

Why the AI Narrative Is Taking Over

The core of the argument is Starlink, SpaceX's satellite internet network. On the surface, it's a connectivity business, beaming broadband down to homes, vehicles, and remote corners of the planet. But increasingly, investors are recognizing that a global, low-latency connectivity network is exactly the kind of infrastructure the AI era is going to depend on.

→ Strait of Hormuz Tensions Spike Tanker Trade: These 2 Stocks Are Set to Benefit

As AI systems become more embedded in everyday devices, vehicles, and industrial applications, the need for reliable connectivity to move data back and forth grows enormously. Starlink is one of the very few networks capable of providing that coverage at scale.

The bulls argue that this could make SpaceX something like an AI infrastructure landlord, with its network and vertical integration allowing it to control data movement and potentially support entirely new compute products over time.

→ Why Wall Street Suddenly Sees SpaceX as an AI Company

This is a view that Wedbush's Dan Ives has been vocal about for some time. He argues that SpaceX should be seen as much more of a data and AI play than a traditional space company, pointing to the strategic value of its network and the growing data demands flowing through it. If that framing gains wider acceptance, it fundamentally changes the lens through which the company is valued to the upside.

sentences.zh: 如果这种框架获得更广泛的接受，它将从根本上改变公司估值的视角。

content_html_zh:
要点

  • 对 SpaceX 感兴趣？ 这是我们更喜欢的五只股票。
  • 自 6 月 12 日 IPO 以来，SpaceX 股价已下跌约 40%，目前交易价格低于 135 美元的上市价格。
  • 华尔街越来越多地将 SpaceX 的 Starlink 网络视为人工智能基础设施，而不仅仅是太空或卫星业务。
  • 在分析师价格目标参差不齐的情况下，SpaceX 于 8 月 6 日发布的首份公开收益报告被视为验证人工智能叙述的关键。

当 SpaceX（纳斯达克股票代码：SPCX）在一个多月前的 6 月 12 日上市时，它成为多年来最受期待的上市，毫无疑问是一家航天公司。 火箭、卫星和火星野心就是故事。 但它在公开市场上市还不到一个月，华尔街就出现了一种不同的说法，它与人工智能 (AI) 的关系远大于太空旅行。

这个时机很有趣，因为该股本身的开局并不顺利。 在 IPO 后的几个交易日中触及 IPO 后高点后，SpaceX 股价已下跌约 40%，目前交易价格低于 135 美元的上市价格。

→ 回购热潮：这 3 家公司在自己的股票上押注数十亿美元

对于一家在上市时引起如此大的兴奋的公司来说，在最初几周内跌破 IPO 价格并不是一个好兆头。 然而，在令人失望的价格走势之下，新兴的投资案例可能比图表显示的更引人注目。

为什么人工智能正在占据主导地位

争论的核心是 SpaceX 的卫星互联网网络 Starlink。 从表面上看，这是一项连接业务，将宽带传输到家庭、车辆和地球上的偏远角落。 但投资者越来越认识到，全球低延迟连接网络正是人工智能时代所依赖的基础设施。

→ 霍尔木兹海峡紧张局势加剧油轮贸易：这两只股票将受益

随着人工智能系统越来越多地嵌入日常设备、车辆和工业应用中，对可靠连接来来回移动数据的需求日益增长。 星链是极少数能够提供大规模覆盖的网络之一。

看多者认为，这可能使 SpaceX 成为人工智能基础设施业主，其网络和垂直整合使其能够控制数据移动，并有可能随着时间的推移支持全新的计算产品。

→ 为什么华尔街突然将 SpaceX 视为一家人工智能公司

韦德布什 (Wedbush) 的丹·艾夫斯 (Dan Ives) 一段时间以来一直直言不讳地表达这一观点。 他认为，与传统的航天公司相比，SpaceX 应该更多地被视为一家数据和人工智能公司，并指出其网络的战略价值以及流经该网络的不断增长的数据需求。 如果这种框架获得更广泛的接受，它将从根本上改变公司估值的视角。
